draft.introduction: Sunzi’s Art of War opens by declaring that warfare is a matter of life and death for the state and must be studied with utmost seriousness. Its principles of strategic calculation, deception, and winning without fighting have influenced military and business thought worldwide.

translation.text: War is a matter of vital importance to the state, the province of life and death, the road to survival or ruin. It is mandatory that it be thoroughly studied. Assess it by means of five factors: the Way, heaven, earth, the commander, and method. All warfare is based on deception. Hence, when able to attack, we must seem unable; when near, make the enemy believe we are far away.
translation.original_text: 兵者，國之大事，死生之地，存亡之道，不可不察也

translator_notes.note: Sunzi’s inclusion of the “Way” (Dao) as the first strategic factor—meaning the moral cause that unites the people with their ruler—shows that the Art of War is not purely amoral Realpolitik. The best general wins without fighting; the best victory is the one that preserves the enemy state intact.
